subject: FIX SONAR ISSUE NESTED TRY BLOCKS
commit_message:
FIX SONAR ISSUE NESTED TRY BLOCKS

refactor the code for nested try blocks and rework
based on comments and fix nested loops complexity

Issue-ID: POLICY-976
Change-Id: Ied02b7838b5e4839a4906f79ff37c4fd6afeccfe
Signed-off-by: Krishnajinka <kris.jinka@samsung.com>

please feel free to comment if you have any ideas on improving the code. 
Also., please feel free to open / add to a new task if you think similar refactoring is needed in someother class.

Thanks Krishnakumar, refactoring this code is a big task and you're making a very good start!

Patch Set 2:

Thank you for the work, what level of testing have you done for these changes (since it is touching critical areas trying to assess potential for breakage) ?

Patch Set 3:

(17 comments)

> Thank you for the work, what level of testing have you done for
 > these changes (since it is touching critical areas trying to assess
 > potential for breakage) ?

Hi Jorge, Usually I have done the local build, with unit tests run, check if the local build is success. I will check the build before making any changes and then after making any changes, that runs unit tests as well., 
In some cases when I run mvn compile test on policy/engine , the test is stuck there for sometime, not sure why. 
But yes, I have not done any runtime test such as deploying this. I dont have that environment. 
Please let me know if any runtime test is necessary, and if there is any environment available that I could use. I will do that as well. 
I am open to suggestions, if you feel that runtime test are necessary, and also it will be great help if you can point me to existing test beds, I could use. 
Yes, to answer your question, I have done unit test, local build, code review, 
Some issues such as modifying the variable access, method access, i check the usage in the module for example in this case policy/engine.

Hi Jorge, Just to add what I answered, I am fully aware of the potential chances of breakage, so far while I have done refactoring into smaller methods, I have used the tools to refactor in IntelliJ so that manual errors could be avoided. 
But its too hard to do this refactoring without properly testing it on runtime, since the code is too long and i am not fully aware of the functions, I fully understand your concern, please let me know your suggestions.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Thanks for the updates.

If you want to get up an ONAP policy environment, try the series of quick starts that are listed on this page:
https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Deploying+and+Debugging+the+Policy+Platform

Patch Set 3:

(2 comments)

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > Thanks for the updates.
 > 
 > If you want to get up an ONAP policy environment, try the series of
 > quick starts that are listed on this page:
 > https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Deploying+and+Debugging+the+Policy+Platform

Thank you Liam Fallon for the link. I followed the link https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/ONAP+Policy+Framework%3A+Standalone+Quick+Start
I ran into error during this step:
cd ~/git/onap/policy/engine/packages/docker/target
docker build -t onap/policy-pe policy-pe

Sending build context to Docker daemon  343.7MB
Step 1/18 : FROM ubuntu:14.04
Get https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/: net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection (Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers)

I thought may be it was certificate issue, I added certificate into keystore, but still error persists., 
I am guessing may be some security software is blocking the download of this docker image. Plz let me know if you have any hints

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Could you put the comment about the quick start on the wiki page and we can take it there? If we put those comments on this review they will get confused with the review and will get lost. Thanks L

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Very good work Krishna. Since you are making modifications then please go ahead and add Modified Copyright notices (Liam, Jorge - I am making the change going forward thanks to Jim pointing out correct way to do this). This way your company gets credit.

Modified Copyright (C) 2018 TechMahindra

Let's make sure Mike Mokry reviews and approves this.

Patch Set 3:

Do you mind if I pull these changes into a lab and run some basic functional test from API and GUI, specifically creating and updating policies.  While I don't see any alarming issues in the code, some of the refactoring affects those critical policy functions. In the past I have seen Junits pass and builds succeed while functionality is still broken during runtime.  I would feel more comfortable if I can run some sanity tests from the API and GUI.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Seems there is no easy way to pull these changes in for testing without potentially causing problems with my own environment.  The code looks good to me, I will test it after its merged to verify functionality.  For future reference I am going to start a wiki in ONAP with some basic level testing for PE functionality that folks can perform quickly to test changes before submitting.

Patch Set 3:

Just waiting for Jorge...

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

Thanks Krishna, yes I agree your changes, in theory, it seems, won't break functionality.

Thank you Mike.  Something along the lines of https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Running+PDP-D+in+Eclipse 
would be very helpful to all new contributors, and for us (trying to prevent breakage on this repo).
Change has been successfully merged by Jorge Hernandez
